catalyst_summary: NBIS rallied ~13% on June 11-12 after Nasdaq announced inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 effective June 22, 2026, triggering anticipated passive ETF inflows. The move builds on a strong Q1 2026 print (revenue +684% YoY to $399M, ARR +674%) and raised FY26 guidance ($3.0-3.4B revenue, $7-9B ARR), plus prior mega-deals with Meta ($27B/5-yr) and Microsoft anchoring the AI cloud capacity ramp.
recent_developments: Nasdaq-100 inclusion announced (effective June 22, 2026); shares jumped ~13% after-hours June 11 and extended gains June 12 on expected index-fund buying.
Q1 2026 results (reported May 13): revenue $399M (+684% YoY, +75% QoQ); adj EBITDA $129.5M vs -$53.7M YoY; AI cloud ~98% of revenue; AI cloud adj EBITDA margin nearly doubled.
FY2026 guidance raised/reaffirmed to $3.0-3.4B revenue and $7-9B ARR; 2026 contracted power lifted to 2.5GW and connected power to 0.8-1GW; capex plan $16-20B.
BofA raised PT to $280 from $240; Citigroup reaffirmed Buy; Street consensus PT clusters in $160-205 with high $280 (BofA) and broader range up to $380.

Meta $27B/5-yr AI infrastructure agreement (signed March 2026; includes $12B guaranteed capacity using NVIDIA Vera Rubin) and multi-billion Microsoft capacity deal (New Jersey DC) continue to underpin demand visibility.
Ongoing insider selling: CTO Danila Shtan sold 15,678 shares (~$3.75M) on June 4; General Counsel sold 5,100 shares May 20; CRO sold 4,500 shares May 15.
key_risks: Premium valuation and heavy capex ($16-20B in 2026) leave little margin for execution slippage; continued insider selling and concentration in a handful of hyperscaler contracts (Meta/Microsoft) create headline risk if any deal terms or delivery timelines slip.
pending_catalysts: Nasdaq-100 inclusion takes effect June 22, 2026 — index-fund rebalancing flows.
Q2 2026 earnings expected mid-August 2026 (based on May 13 Q1 cadence) — focus on ARR ramp toward $7-9B target.
Capacity milestones: connected power ramp toward 0.8-1GW target and progress on Vineland, NJ data center start-up later in 2026.
Potential additional hyperscaler/enterprise capacity contracts following Meta/Microsoft template.
